evals.prompt: I want to add BarcodeCapture to my .NET for iOS app. Here's my empty ViewController: EmptyViewController.cs. I need to scan EAN-13 and Code 128 barcodes for a retail app.
evals.expected_output: The skill reads integration.md, fetches the latest Scandit NuGet versions, writes complete BarcodeCapture integration code into EmptyViewController.cs (BarcodeCapture.Create, IBarcodeCaptureListener implementation, Camera, DataCaptureView with bounds, BarcodeCaptureOverlay, ViewWillAppear/ViewWillDisappear lifecycle, frameData.Dispose()), and shows the setup checklist (NuGet packages, NSCameraUsageDescription, license key).

assertions.text: Setup checklist is shown and mentions adding the Scandit.DataCapture.Barcode and Scandit.DataCapture.Core NuGet packages
Setup checklist mentions adding NSCameraUsageDescription to Info.plist
A license key placeholder string is present
DataCaptureContext.ForLicenseKey( is used to create the context
BarcodeCapture.RecommendedCameraSettings is used (as a property, not VideoResolution.Auto)
Camera.GetDefaultCamera() is used to obtain the camera
camera.ApplySettingsAsync(BarcodeCapture.RecommendedCameraSettings) is called
dataCaptureContext.SetFrameSourceAsync( is called with the camera
BarcodeCaptureSettings.Create() is used to construct settings (not new BarcodeCaptureSettings())
Symbology.Ean13Upca is enabled
Symbology.Code128 is enabled
No symbologies other than Ean13Upca and Code128 are enabled
BarcodeCapture.Create(dataCaptureContext, settings) is used to create the mode (not BarcodeCapture.ForDataCaptureContext or new BarcodeCapture())
barcodeCapture.AddListener( is called (or barcodeCapture.BarcodeScanned += is wired)
DataCaptureView.Create(dataCaptureContext, this.View!.Bounds) (or this.View.Bounds) is used — the second argument is a CGRect / bounds
BarcodeCaptureOverlay.Create(barcodeCapture, dataCaptureView) is called
The ViewController implements IBarcodeCaptureListener (or subscribes to BarcodeScanned via EventHandler<BarcodeCaptureEventArgs>)
OnBarcodeScanned (or the BarcodeScanned event handler) reads session.NewlyRecognizedBarcode
frameData.Dispose() is called inside OnBarcodeScanned (including on the early-return path)
frameData.Dispose() is called inside OnSessionUpdated
OnSessionUpdated, OnObservationStarted, and OnObservationStopped are implemented if using the listener interface
barcodeCapture.Enabled = false is set inside OnBarcodeScanned
camera.SwitchToDesiredStateAsync(FrameSourceState.On) is called in ViewWillAppear (or equivalent)
camera.SwitchToDesiredStateAsync(FrameSourceState.Off) is called in ViewWillDisappear (or equivalent)
DispatchQueue.MainQueue.DispatchAsync is used to dispatch UI work in OnBarcodeScanned

evals.prompt: Add barcode scanning to my existing .NET iOS ViewController. Here's the ViewController: EmptyViewController.cs. I want to scan QR codes and Data Matrix. When a barcode is scanned, log the barcode data.
evals.expected_output: The skill integrates BarcodeCapture into EmptyViewController.cs with QR and Data Matrix symbologies enabled. OnBarcodeScanned logs the barcode data via Console.WriteLine or NSLog. Shows the setup checklist.

assertions.text: Symbology.Qr is enabled
Symbology.DataMatrix is enabled
No symbologies other than Qr and DataMatrix are enabled
BarcodeCapture.Create( is used
OnBarcodeScanned (or BarcodeScanned event) is implemented and accesses barcode.Data
The scan result is logged (Console.WriteLine, System.Diagnostics.Debug.WriteLine, NSLog, or similar)
frameData.Dispose() is called inside OnBarcodeScanned
ViewWillAppear turns the camera on
ViewWillDisappear turns the camera off
Setup checklist mentions Scandit.DataCapture.Barcode and Scandit.DataCapture.Core NuGet packages
Setup checklist mentions NSCameraUsageDescription

evals.prompt: When a barcode is scanned in my .NET iOS BarcodeCapture app, I want to disable scanning, look up the barcode in a remote API (an async Task call), and re-enable scanning when the lookup completes. Wire this up safely.
evals.expected_output: The skill sets barcodeCapture.Enabled = false at the start of OnBarcodeScanned, awaits the async lookup, and re-enables scanning in a finally block. UI updates are dispatched via DispatchQueue.MainQueue.DispatchAsync. frameData.Dispose() is always called.

assertions.text: barcodeCapture.Enabled = false is set inside OnBarcodeScanned (or the BarcodeScanned event handler) before the async work
An async/await call or a Task continuation is used to perform the lookup
barcodeCapture.Enabled = true is set after the lookup completes (typically in a finally block)
DispatchQueue.MainQueue.DispatchAsync is used to dispatch UI work after the async lookup
frameData.Dispose() is still called in OnBarcodeScanned even when the lookup is async (typically in an outer finally block)
Existing integration code (DataCaptureContext, barcodeCapture, camera, listener/event) is preserved

evals.prompt: I want to suppress the default beep and vibration when a barcode is scanned in my .NET iOS app — silent scanning. Update the BarcodeCapture feedback.
evals.expected_output: The skill reassigns barcodeCapture.Feedback.Success to a Feedback constructed with null vibration and null sound (or replaces the whole BarcodeCaptureFeedback with one whose Success has no audio or haptic effect).

assertions.text: barcodeCapture.Feedback.Success is assigned to a Feedback with no sound and no vibration (Feedback(vibration: null, sound: null) or equivalent)
Existing integration code (DataCaptureContext, barcodeCapture, camera, listener) is preserved

evals.prompt: Add a square viewfinder to my .NET iOS BarcodeCapture preview so users can see where to aim.
evals.expected_output: The skill creates a RectangularViewfinder with RectangularViewfinderStyle.Square and assigns it to overlay.Viewfinder.

assertions.text: A RectangularViewfinder is constructed with RectangularViewfinderStyle.Square
The viewfinder is assigned to BarcodeCaptureOverlay.Viewfinder (overlay.Viewfinder = ...)
Existing integration code (DataCaptureContext, barcodeCapture, camera, listener) is preserved

evals.prompt: I need to filter scans so duplicates of the same code within 500 milliseconds are ignored. Update my .NET iOS BarcodeCapture settings.
evals.expected_output: The skill sets settings.CodeDuplicateFilter = TimeSpan.FromMilliseconds(500) on the BarcodeCaptureSettings instance (using TimeSpan, not a bare integer, double, or TimeInterval).

assertions.text: CodeDuplicateFilter is set to TimeSpan.FromMilliseconds(500) (not a bare integer 500, not a double 0.5, not TimeInterval)
CodeDuplicateFilter is set before BarcodeCapture.Create( or via barcodeCapture.ApplySettingsAsync
Existing integration code (DataCaptureContext, barcodeCapture, camera, listener) is preserved

evals.prompt: My .NET iOS BarcodeCapture preview is frozen or stuttering. Help me diagnose and fix it.
evals.expected_output: The skill points out that frameData.Dispose() must be called at the end of every OnBarcodeScanned and OnSessionUpdated callback (including early-return paths). This is the canonical fix on iOS.

assertions.text: The skill identifies that frameData.Dispose() must be called in OnBarcodeScanned and OnSessionUpdated
The skill explains that failing to dispose the frame causes a frozen, non-responsive, or stuttering preview
The fix is applied to the existing OnBarcodeScanned and/or OnSessionUpdated callback
